subject: Mathematics
question_text: Which of the following statements is not true?
options: When two positive integers are added, we always get a positive integer.
When two negative integers are added we always get a negative integer.
When a positive integer and a negative integer is added we always get a negative integer.
Additive inverse of an integer 2 is (–2) and additive inverse of (–2) is 2.
answer_key: When a positive integer and a negative integer is added we always get a negative integer.
answer_explanation: Adding a positive and a negative integer can result in a positive integer, a negative integer, or zero (e.g., 5 + (–3) = 2).

question_text: Water level in a well was 20m below ground level. During rainy season, rain water collected in different water tanks was drained into the well and the water level rises 5 m above the previous level. The wall of the well is 1m 20 cm high and a pulley is fixed at a height of 80 cm. Raghu wants to draw water from the well. The minimum length of the rope that he can use is
answer_explanation: Initial level = –20 m. Rises by 5 m, so new level = –15 m (15 m below ground). Wall is 1.2 m, pulley is 0.8 m above wall. Total height above ground = 1.2 + 0.8 = 2 m. Minimum rope length = 15 m + 2 m = 17 m.

subject: Mathematics
question_text:
An atom consists of charged particles called electrons and protons. Each proton has a charge of +1 and each electron has a charge of –1. Remember number of electrons is equal to number of protons, while answering these questions:
(a) What is the charge on an atom?
(b) What will be the charge on an atom if it loses an electron?
(c) What will be the charge on an atom if it gains an electron?
answer_explanation: (a) Since # electrons = # protons, (+1) + (-1) = 0. (b) Losing an electron (-1) leaves a net positive charge of +1. (c) Gaining an electron gives an extra -1 charge.
provenance_excerpt: An atom consists of charged particles called electrons and protons. Each proton has a charge of +1 and each electron has

subject: Mathematics
question_text:
Remembering that 1AD came immediately after 1BC, while solving these problems take 1BC as –1 and 1AD as +1.
(a) The Greeco-Roman era started in 330 BC and ended in 395 AD. How long did this era last?
(b) Bhaskaracharya was born in 1114 AD and died in 1185 AD. What was his age when he died?
(c) Turks ruled Egypt in 1517 AD and Queen Nefertis ruled Egypt about 2900 years before the Turks ruled. In what year did she rule?
(d) Greek mathematician Archimedes lived between 287 BC and 212 BC and Aristotle lived between 380 BC and 322 BC. Who lived during an earlier period?
answer_explanation: (a) From 330 BC to 395 AD (no year zero) -> 395 - (-330) - 1 = 724. (b) 1185 - 1114 = 71. (c) 1517 - 2900 = -1383 -> year 1384 BC. (d) Aristotle (380 BC is earlier than 287 BC).
provenance_excerpt:

subject: Mathematics
question_text: Sana and Fatima participated in an apple race. The race was conducted in 6 parts. In the first part, Sana won by 10 seconds. In the second part she lost by 1 minute, then won by 20 seconds, lost by 25 seconds, lost by 37 seconds, and won by 12 seconds. Who won the race finally?
answer_explanation: Sana's margins: +10, -60, +20, -25, -37, +12. Net = 10 - 60 + 20 - 25 - 37 + 12 = -80 seconds. Since it's negative, Sana lost by 80 seconds. Thus Fatima won.
